free_text: Evaluation of the cross-sections for the reactions
and Zr-90(n,2n)Zr-89
Evaluation
Evaluation primarily based on experimentally measured
cross sections. all data sets were critically reviewed
and obviously erroneous data sets were disregarded.
if necessary, the data were renormalized to recent
values of standard cross sections and decay schemes.
(The experimental references considered are given
under REL-REF for the individual reactions.
The cross sections were evaluated for energy groups
between 0.1 MeV and 1 MeV wide, the width depending
on both the slope of the excitation functions and the
density of the available experimental data.
NOTE:
Although the evaluated cross sections were determined
for the centre energy of the respective energy groups,
in the publication the  LOWER LIMIT is given as
independent variable (Group-energy) (Table No.4 for
each reaction). For the present compilation those
group-energies were converted to the centre-energies
for each energy group.
Relative correlation matrices (describing correlations
between the uncertainties of the cross sections at
different energies and between uncertainties for
different reactions), also in the form of "relative
covariances" required for ENDF/B-5 format.
For each cross section an uncertainty - on
a 1 sigma confidence level - was derived taking into
account both the errors given by the experimentalists
and the general consistency of the experimental data.
Table No.4 of Physik Daten/Physics Data 13-1
(1979), work partly supported by IAEA Research
Contract No.2047.
